articles.title: Longer routes, higher costs, 26,000 cancellations: US-Iran conflict hits Indian airlines
articles.summary: Indian airlines have cancelled approximately 26,000 international flights due to Middle East airspace restrictions. These disruptions have led to flight diversions and delays, impacting airline operations significantly.

articles.title: Will employees finally get paid faster after resignation? Understanding labour codes and full & final settlement
articles.summary: For an employee, the last salary from the previous organisation is not just a payroll entry. It may be needed for rent, relocation expenses, family commitments, education fees, loan repayments or simply to manage the gap before the first salary from the new employer

articles.title: India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks around 900 km in five days
articles.summary: India's first hydrogen fuel cell train has covered 900 kilometers on its initial operational route. This new train runs on hydrogen fuel cells, producing only water vapor and heat. Passenger fares remain consistent with ordinary diesel services on this pilot project.

articles.title: Who is Ashiss Kumar Dash, the new CEO-designate at IT major Infosys?
articles.summary: Ashiss Kumar Dash currently serves as the global head of a diversified business portfolio spanning more than 12 industry verticals.

articles.title: India’s first private rocket reaches orbit: The space race has just changed forever
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 marks a historic shift as India’s first privately developed orbital rocket reaches space, opening a new era for startups, innovation and commercial launches.

articles.title: Terminated while recovering? Gurugram woman’s post triggers debate over employee rights
articles.summary: After a sudden surgery and an approved leave, her dismissal has now sparked a wider debate on workplace rights. What started as a medical emergency soon turned into a bigger question!

articles.title: From runway to cliff: How a dead aircraft turned into a luxury escape
articles.summary: A retired Boeing 737 gets a stunning second life as a luxury cliff villa in Bali. Discover how an abandoned aircraft was transformed into a futuristic stay above the Indian Ocean.

articles.title: That white box on your roof isn’t useless — it may be bringing your internet
articles.summary: That blinking white box on rooftops is not useless — it may be delivering your internet. Discover how UBR technology helps ISPs expand connectivity without traditional fibre cables.

articles.title: Samsung’s hidden strategy: Turning smartphone users into financial customers
articles.summary: Samsung is moving beyond smartphones with Galaxy Card, combining payments, cashback and Samsung Wallet to build a stronger digital ecosystem and turn users into long-term financial customers.

articles.title: Couple paid ₹1.2 lakh for a Lakshadweep trip, then ₹21,500 extra : What happened next sent them to consumer court
articles.summary: After paying ₹1.2 lakh for a holiday membership and ₹21,500 extra before departure, the couple claimed they did not receive the accommodation promised in their Lakshadweep package.

articles.title: CWG 2026 medal race: The Indian stars who could rewrite history
articles.summary: Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu and India’s top athletes enter CWG 2026 chasing glory. Explore the medal hopes, fierce rivalries and stars who could create history in Glasgow.

articles.title: From Haldiram’s to Lakshmi Mittal: Who made millions from Skyroot, India’s first private space unicorn
articles.summary: This week, Skyroot Aerospace scripted history with the successful launch of Vikram-1, India's first privately developed orbital rocket, from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota under Mission Aagaman.

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy Glasses are officially here: Gemini AI, 12MP camera, 9-hour battery, Snapdragon AR1 Gen 1 — all specs and price
articles.summary: Samsung Galaxy Glasses officially revealed at Galaxy Unpacked July 22 — Snapdragon AR1, Gemini AI, 12MP camera, 9-hour battery, Warby Parker and Gentle Monster frames. Fall 2026 launch.

articles.title: 'Speaking up gets eyeballs but risky for business': 1% Club founder Sharan Hegde amid CJP protests
articles.summary: In an Instagram post shared on July 22, Hegde addressed the protests linked to the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and the demands for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

articles.title: Travelling on Vande Bharat sleeper for the first time? Here is what you get
articles.summary: Planning your first Vande Bharat Sleeper journey? Here is what to expect from its three AC classes, redesigned berths, regional meals, luggage areas and charging ports.

articles.title: Axis Bank's MF arm acquires Rs 12,000 crore PMS business from securities arm
articles.summary: Axis Asset Management Company is acquiring Axis Securities' portfolio management services business. This strategic move significantly expands their alternative investments platform and investor base.

articles.title: Beyond Hormuz: Gulf is spending billions to find new routes to global markets
articles.summary: Gulf nations are accelerating pipeline projects to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. These new routes aim to move oil without relying on the narrow waterway. Several major pipeline projects are under construction or being planned.

articles.title: Mumbai to Ahmedabad by train? Check this first — 15 services cancelled, 3 short-terminated, 2 diverted on July 23
articles.summary: Western Railway cancels 15 trains on July 23 due to flooding between Gholvad-Umbergaon. Vande Bharat, Shatabdi, Surat Intercity, Bandra-Ramnagar among cancelled services. Check before you travel.

articles.title: Noida Metro Aqua Line extension: 7.5 km, 5 new stations to connect 5 lakh Greater Noida West residents — full details
articles.summary: Noida Metro Aqua Line extension: 7.5 km, 5 stations from Sector 51 to Kisan Chowk, Rs 1,500 crore, connecting 5 lakh Greater Noida West residents to Delhi Metro and Jewar Airport.

articles.title: 'What's your company's Hormuz?': The new management concept born from a global chokepoint
articles.summary: The analogy is straightforward. Just as disruption in the Strait of Hormuz can ripple across global energy prices, freight costs, inflation and economic activity, businesses too often rely heavily on one crucial link in their operations.

articles.title: 'Day 2 was a very short conversation': Delhi founder on firing employee within 2 days of appointment; netizens divided
articles.summary: A Delhi-based founder’s LinkedIn post about firing a new employee on the second day of work has sparked a heated debate online.

articles.title: What is 'Dharavi Didi'? Adani launches AI-powered chatbot to provide verified information on Dharavi redevelopment
articles.summary: Adani Navbharat Developers has launched Dharavi Didi, an AI companion for verified project information. The platform is meant to improve access, transparency and trust for Dharavikars during redevelopment.

articles.title: DDA's biggest housing deal of 2026: Ready-to-move freehold flats in Delhi at 25% off, near upcoming Metro — step by step guide
articles.summary: DDA Karmajeevi Awaas Yojana 2026 offers 1,224 ready-to-move flats in Narela at 25% off. Registration opens July 24. Booking from August 15 on first come, first served basis.

articles.title: AI-led data centre boom is boosting fibre optic cable demand, says HFCL MD Mahendra Nahata
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is emerging as a key driver of global demand for fibre optic cables, with hyperscale data centre expansion boosting orders worldwide.

articles.title: India's new road trip map: Mumbai-Pune busiest road trip route but Tier-2 routes are growing fastest - Report
articles.summary: Mumbai to Pune, Delhi to Agra, Bengaluru to Mysuru, Lucknow to Kanpur, and Ahmedabad to Vadodara remained India's highest-volume intercity corridors during the quarter

articles.title: Rupee rises to 96.49 against US dollar, rising oil prices limit gains
articles.summary: The Indian rupee saw a slight gain against the US dollar on Thursday. Rising crude oil prices continue to exert pressure on the currency. The Reserve Bank of India's intervention is expected to limit sharper losses.

articles.title: More pulp, less tax: Govt panel hits the sweet spot with 'aam' proposal to help Totapuri farmers
articles.summary: The government proposes a 5% GST rate for mango drinks with 22-25% real pulp. This move aims to revive Totapuri mango pulp demand and improve beverage quality. An expert committee studied Totapuri price declines and suggested solutions for farmers.

articles.title: Stock market today: NSE Nifty50 opens below 24,000, BSE Sensex sheds over 350 points
Oil prices touch six week high as Strait of Hormuz is 'completely closed'
articles.summary: Crude oil prices surged as Middle East conflict escalated on Thursday. Brent crude rose above $96 a barrel and WTI climbed past $88. Fresh attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea fueled supply concerns. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed control of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Under-recoveries push HPCL, BPCL into losses
OpenAI's rogue AI breach may bolster India's case for frontier model access
Q1 bank credit flow surges 10x as market mop-up collapses
Improving liquidity helping robust growth in credit: RBI
articles.summary: India's economy shows resilience amid global uncertainties and fragile geopolitics. Liquidity conditions have improved, supporting robust credit growth across the nation. The external sector remains steady, with foreign investment inflows strengthening its outlook.

articles.title: Pharma companies in a flux over Trump's 200% tariff move on generics
Temasek widens scope of India investments
articles.summary: Temasek is broadening its Indian investment focus beyond traditional sectors. The investor will now target industrials and artificial intelligence opportunities. Its India portfolio has grown significantly, reaching $42 billion by March 2026.

articles.title: Trump's 25% tariff hits Brazil: Trade tensions deepen as billions in exports at risk
'Aam'ezing voyage: UP's mangoes make first sea journey to Dubai
articles.summary: Indian mangoes reached Dubai via a 25-day sea journey. Nearly ninety percent of the fruit remained market-ready after transit. This successful shipment paves the way for cheaper and larger mango exports. The new protocol reduces reliance on expensive air freight for shipments.

articles.title: Trump's 200% tariff plan may be a bitter pill for US consumers; Dr Reddy's warns of costlier medicines
articles.summary: The United States plans significant tariffs on imported generic medicines. These duties will rise to 200% in the coming years. Dr Reddy's Laboratories anticipates higher medicine prices for American consumers.

articles.title: US stock market today: Wall Street drifts in mixed trading; oil prices rise another 3%
articles.summary: The S&P 500 slipped 0.2%, despite a majority of its constituent stocks trading higher. Artificial intelligence-related stocks have remained the key driver of recent market volatility.

articles.title: BPCL posts Rs 3,962 crore Q1 loss as high crude prices squeeze fuel margins
articles.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd reported a significant net loss for the April-June quarter. Elevated crude oil prices and regulated fuel rates severely impacted marketing margins. The company incurred substantial LPG under-recoveries and unpaid subsidy dues.
